en_title: 2005: Poet-Lover: Kennedy Space Center Florida USA
en_body:
Hoàng-Ân.........................................................................................
.........................................................................................................


2005: Poet-Lover: Kennedy Space Center Florida USA


The space-flight path, the spacecraft of Epcot gardens
Yet curves of Calculus, Trigonometry sweep beyond the border:
Hovering, performing through my life,
Yet far-off formulas, dust over dreams of textbook-blackboard!
Canvas-shoe prints, thick heels, easy feet strolling:
Civilization has landed, yet warships, shuttle-flowers,
Radiant, polished, the body of the dream-fashioned vessel
Tears through the raging fire of Matter: the mystic universe reels...
Here, the classroom appoints the hour of cap and gown
Dream of science-board fulfilling the will of books, long ago,...
,Long ago,: the folds of áo-dài, two fragrant panels, shyly closed
Perfect embroidery, the piping line of oil-drilled wells,
And cities, the Frank Lloyd Wright museum-palace,
Escher paintings, the contest of artistic concepts
between Matisse and Pablo Picasso****
Ayn Rand's "Atlas Shrugged" of Objectivism
Leonardo da Vinci and USA helicopter "Mona Lisa" of the 20th century
The Great Library of the free superpower: Library of Congress:

Ancestors' Will: now my feet stride the broad road: Play..!!
Play to fulfill the spirit of a child of Việt Dragon,
From Nguyễn Trãi's sacred books; the Prince's Strategies of War...
Now on strange earth: enrich the Heart, review History, tunnel through Scripture
The tangerine of Hoài-Nam: seed multiplying toward future harvests..!!

Grandmother's six-eight rhymes lull,
Home garden, plum, jasmine trembling branches, heavy noon...
Amid the vast yard under blazing sun, warships and aircraft:
Spacecraft, forefinger, traces a poem of Freedom:

 A daughter, yet no common-hearted one,
 In foreign land the bell tolls one fragrant rhyme:
 "A hundred years within this human realm...
 Word Talent, word Destiny..." virtue crimson, blessing within.
 "Ponder well, all things are Heaven's,
 ...To the noble (may be granted) the noble share!"*
 Be an example, silver age among green strands,
 Lead the flock of children far and wide to become Human...
 Mother returns, gathering the word "Yielding"**
 Giving her child a splendid shard, a level mirror,
 Cap, scarf, robe flowing to slippered heels
 School gate closes: midnight lamp shines on the page,
 ...Among equations solving for the unknown,
 Tucked fragrant, whose hand copied the folk-song "...each evening..."***

 Each evening, again remembering each evening,
 Night stars over old pond, mud grieving the blurred star,
 À ơi...who sings beyond the river,
 Foreign city's realm...still chants the ancient longing!
 In daylight suddenly remembering History, long ago,
 A Mother's broad heels upon the roads of study and examination..!!

 Tending the lamp, remembering the sacred firelight,
 Long-ago night, Châu-Trí studied beneath a poor hut's pierced roof:
 Fireflies were also toys for the child,
 Kept in a little jar, laughter ringing through innocent dreams:
 ...Sacred books of the poor scholar,
 Robe untouched by mulberry-field scandal, candle shining on lessons,
 Mother now, silver leaning through her hair
 Tireless in the word Duty: illuminating her child's young Heart:

"...A fragrant torch bears Lady Triệu's name in peace,
 Sheathing the sword, taking up the books again, long ago, tireless..!!"



(Sending words of shared joy to the esteemed retired women poets, heroic daughters of Việt-Nam,
 for the festival season of "The Joyous Day of Compassionate Motherhood" in the United States and throughout
 the whole globe (13/05/2012)
........................................................................................................
* Nguyễn Du, The Tale of Kiều
** "Yielding" as in the saying, "The yielding overcomes the hard, the weak overcomes the strong."
*** Folk verses describing a woman married far from home, who often at
 dusk stands at the back gate remembering her old mother in the homeland, as in
 "each evening I stand at the back gate...looking toward Mother's country, my heart aches ninefold."
****Pablo Picasso said: "It takes a long time to be young!"
 (Time must ripen before it brings forth (the attitude/the bearing)
 of youth.")*-)
note: The poem invokes Nguyễn Du’s Tale of Kiều, the Daoist maxim that yielding overcomes hardness, Lady Triệu, and a folk lament sung by a daughter married far from her mother.
